●Live Updates
◆🔴 BNS 2023 — Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita fully effective from July 1, 2024 (Replaces IPC 1860)◆⚡ BNSS 2023 — Zero FIR provision now mandatory across all states◆🔵 Supreme Court: Right to Privacy is a Fundamental Right under Article 21◆📋 BSA 2023 — Electronic evidence admissible without physical certification◆🏠 RERA Amendment — Builder penalty doubled, homebuyers rights strengthened◆⚖️ Motor Vehicles Act — Hit-and-run compensation increased to ₹2 Lakh◆📜 RTI Act — Response time reduced to 15 days for life/liberty matters◆🛡️ Companies Act Amendment 2024 — New CSR compliance norms effective Q1 2025◆👨‍⚖️ Fast Track Courts — POCSO cases must conclude within 6 months◆🔔 Consumer Protection — E-commerce return policy rules strengthened 2024

The Great Law Library

Featured Acts & Laws

Browse India's most important legislation — fully indexed, searchable, and explained clearly.

Supreme Law

Constitution of India

1950 · 448 Articles

The supreme law of India. Fundamental Rights, DPSP, and the structure of government.

Read Full Act
New IPC

BNS (New IPC)

2023 · 358 Sections

Replaces IPC 1860. Modernized criminal law with new offences and community service as punishment.

Read Full Act
New CrPC

BNSS (New CrPC)

2023 · 531 Sections

Replaces CrPC 1973. Zero FIR, trial in absentia, and enhanced victim rights.

Read Full Act
New IEA

BSA (New IEA)

2023 · 170 Sections

Replaces Indian Evidence Act 1872. Electronic evidence and digital records recognized.

Read Full Act
Corporate

Corporate Laws

2013 · 470 Sections

Governs incorporation, management, and winding up of companies. Includes CSR, NCLT.

Read Full Act
Civil Law

Civil Laws

1908 · 158 Sections

Procedural law for civil courts. Governs how civil suits are filed, tried, and decided.

Read Full Act
View Complete Law Library
Citizen Empowerment

Legal Awareness for Every Citizen

Understanding your constitutional and civil rights is the first step towards justice.

Fundamental Rights

Articles 12–35: Right to Equality, Freedom, Against Exploitation, Religion, Culture & Education, and Constitutional Remedies.

Learn More

Property Rights

Right to property, tenant rights, RERA protections, and land acquisition laws explained simply.

Learn More

Labour Rights

Minimum wage, PF, gratuity, maternity benefits, and protection against wrongful termination.

Learn More

Women's Rights

Domestic Violence Act, Dowry Prohibition, POCSO, Maternity Benefit Act, and equal pay rights.

Learn More

Right to Education

RTE Act 2009: Free and compulsory education for children aged 6–14. Admission rights.

Learn More

Consumer Rights

Consumer Protection Act 2019: Right to safety, information, choice, redressal, and consumer courts.

Learn More

Rights During Arrest

Section 50 BNSS: Right to know grounds, right to bail, right to lawyer, and medical examination.

Learn More

Digital Rights

IT Act 2000, Data Protection Bill, right to privacy online, and cyber crime protections.

Learn More
View All Rights
Landmark Judgments

Landmark Judgments

Cases that shaped Indian law and society. Understand the precedents that define your rights today.

Constitutional

Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala

Established the Basic Structure Doctrine — Parliament cannot amend the Constitution to destroy its basic structure.

1973 CJ S.M. Sikri (13-Judge Bench)
Read Judgment
Fundamental Rights

Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India

Expanded Article 21 — procedure must be fair, just, and reasonable. Linked Articles 14, 19, and 21.

1978 CJ M.H. Beg
Read Judgment
Privacy

K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India

Right to Privacy declared a Fundamental Right under Article 21. Landmark judgment in the digital age.

2017 9-Judge Bench
Read Judgment
Civil Rights

Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India

Section 377 IPC decriminalized for consensual same-sex relations between adults. Historic LGBTQ+ rights judgment.

2018 CJ Dipak Misra
Read Judgment
Browse All Cases
